/**
 * Finds the index of the largest element in nums <= X.
 * Returns -1 if all elements are > X.
 * Assumes nums is sorted.
 * @param {number[]} nums 
 * @param {number} X 
 * @returns {number} index
 */
function upper_bound_index(nums, X) {
    let low = 0;
    let high = nums.length - 1;
    let result = -1;

    while (low <= high) {
        const mid = Math.floor((low + high) / 2);
        if (nums[mid] <= X) {
            result = mid; // This is a potential candidate
            low = mid + 1; // Try to find a larger one on the right
        } else {
            high = mid - 1; // Too big, search left
        }
    }
    return result;
}


function solve(nums, queries) {
    const N = nums.length;
    
    // 1. Precompute E_count: E_count[i] = count of even numbers in nums[0..i]
    const E_count = new Array(N);
    let currentCount = 0;
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        if (nums[i] % 2 === 0) {
            currentCount++;
        }
        E_count[i] = currentCount;
    }

    const results = [];

    // Maximum possible M we need to check. Since k <= 10^9, M needs to be at least 10^9.
    // An upper bound of 2 * 10^9 + 2 is safe.
    const MAX_M = 2000000002; 

    for (const query of queries) {
        const [l, r, k] = query;

        // Binary Search for the smallest M such that M - C(2M) >= k
        let lowM = 1;
        let highM = MAX_M;
        let bestM = MAX_M;

        while (lowM <= highM) {
            const M = Math.floor((lowM + highM) / 2);
            const X = 2 * M;

            // Calculate C(X): count of even numbers in nums[l..r] that are <= X
            
            // Step 1: Find index j = largest index such that nums[j] <= X
            const j = upper_bound_index(nums, X);

            // Step 2: Determine the effective right boundary R'
            // We are constrained by query boundary r AND value boundary j
            const R_prime = Math.min(r, j);

            let C_X = 0;
            if (R_prime >= l) {
                // Count even numbers in nums[l..R_prime]
                const countUpToRPrime = E_count[R_prime];
                
                let countBeforeL = 0;
                if (l > 0) {
                    countBeforeL = E_count[l - 1];
                }
                
                C_X = countUpToRPrime - countBeforeL;
            }
            
            // Check the condition: M - C_X >= k
            if (M - C_X >= k) {
                // M might be the answer, try smaller M
                bestM = M;
                highM = M - 1;
            } else {
                // M is too small, need more evens remaining
                lowM = M + 1;
            }
        }
        
        const ans = 2 * bestM;
        results.push(ans);
    }

    return results;
}
```
